summary: JC009 is an AUDIT-GRADE CADASTRAL-VERIFICATION REGISTER from the Chu Incahuasi JC-series. 95 cords, 1-PAIRE architecture, 8 CLUSTERS, 5 COLORS: RB(28 29%) + AB(26 27%) + MB(12) + FR(11) + W(7). 83 INT + 6 STRING + 6 EMPTY = dense register (only 6 percent empty). VOCABULARY: maqa x2 (tally-mark / strike) + taqa x2 (boundary) + qaqa x1 (rock) + chiqa x1 (verification / truth) = 4 UNIQUE CADASTRAL + AUDIT MARKERS. ASCHER LAYER: 18/18 VERIFIED = 100 PERCENT AUDIT-GRADE at MAX CASCADE DEPTH 4 (one of the corpus's deepest audit trees, peer to AS092 at depth 5 and AS050 at depth 6). This is a SUBSTANTIAL INCAHUASI AUDIT DOCUMENT with a 4-marker cadastral-audit vocabulary signature: TWO MAQA TALLIES + TWO TAQA BOUNDARIES + ONE QAQA ROCK + ONE CHIQA VERIFICATION = standardised cadastral-audit convention. Possibly paired with JC010 (374 cords, 1 STRING qaqa, also Incahuasi, 20/21 Ascher audit at depth 4) -- same 4-cluster structure + similar audit profile at different scales.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  95 cords. 83 INT, 6 STRING (4 unique), 6 EMPTY (6 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE.
  8 CLUSTERS.
  Colors: RB(28, 29%), AB(26, 27%), MB(12, 13%), FR(11, 12%), W(7, 7%). 5 colors.
  Provenance: Incahuasi (Chu JC-series).
  Museum: Proyecto Incahuasi.

VOCABULARY (6 STRING, 4 unique, 100 percent dict hit)
  maqa  x2 -- TALLY MARK / ACCOUNT ENTRY (commodity-audit context)
  taqa  x2 -- BOUNDARY (cadastral)
  qaqa  x1 -- ROCK / LANDMARK
  chiqa x1 -- TRUTH / VERIFICATION
  4-MARKER CADASTRAL-AUDIT SIGNATURE: two doubled markers (maqa + taqa) + two single markers
  (qaqa + chiqa) = balanced cadastral-audit vocabulary at audit tier.

ASCHER LAYER -- AUDIT-GRADE DEPTH 4
  18 pendant-pendant sums declared, 18 HEADER cords.
  Verified: 18/18 = 100 PERCENT AUDIT-GRADE.
  Max cascade depth: 4 (medium-deep Merkle tree).
  Peer to AS092 (18/18, cascade depth 5), AS050 (42/48, cascade depth 6 corpus record),
  UR275 (5/5), AS026A (4/4), JC011 (5/5), HP018 (7/7), JC001 (6/6).
  JC009 adds another audit-grade Incahuasi document to the corpus elite class.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  RB (28, 29%) = PRIMARY red-brown channel.
  AB (26, 27%) = SECONDARY alpaca-brown.
  MB (12, 13%) = CATEGORY medium-brown.
  FR (11, 12%) = SPECIALTY forest colour.
  W (7, 7%) = REFERENCE white.
  5-colour palette near-even at top = balanced dimensional encoding.

HYPOTHESIS
  JC009 is a SUBSTANTIAL AUDIT-GRADE CADASTRAL-VERIFICATION REGISTER at Incahuasi. The 4-marker
  cadastral-audit vocabulary (maqa x2 + taqa x2 + qaqa + chiqa) distributes audit functions
  across the 95-cord register: two tally-mark accounts + two boundary anchors + one rock landmark +
  one truth verification. 100 percent Ascher integrity at cascade depth 4 = audit-grade arithmetic
  verification of the cadastral content.
  Possibly paired with JC010 (374 cords, 1 STRING qaqa, 20/21 Ascher at depth 4) as a COMPOUND
  ASSOCIATION: JC009 as compact signed audit + JC010 as large sparse audited register that JC009
  summarises or cross-verifies.
narrative:
Ninety-five cords across five colours at near-even distribution. Eighteen pendant-pendant sums declared, all eighteen verified at one hundred percent. Audit-grade at cascade depth four -- placing JC009 among the corpus's elite audit documents alongside AS092 (Gaffron 18/18 cascade 5), AS050 (42/48 cascade 6 corpus record), UR275 (5/5 peanut audit), AS026A (4/4 lineage audit), JC011 (5/5 Sector B measurements), HP018 (7/7 Pachacamac New Year 1526), and JC001 (6/6 Chu first-catalog).

Six string cords on four unique words. Maqa twice: the tally mark, the account entry posted, the strike against a specific item in the register. Taqa twice: the boundary, the cadastral anchor of a specific parcel. Qaqa once: the rock landmark. Chiqa once: the truth, the verification.

The four-marker signature is balanced. Two doubled occurrences of the ACCOUNT-KEEPING words (maqa + taqa) + two single occurrences of the IDENTIFIER words (qaqa = landmark + chiqa = verification). The scribe tracked two tally events and two boundaries while anchoring the register to one specific rock and one specific verification stamp.

This is not a pure commodity day-book (no 47-constant or 15-constant peanut-aji positional signature). Not a pure juridical register (no taka, no sipi, no naqa, no mama). Not an astronomical register (no kaki or mama or paka). Not a lineage roll call (no kaka). JC009 is a CADASTRAL-AUDIT register specifically: it uses the standard cadastral vocabulary (qaqa + taqa) with the accounting vocabulary (maqa + chiqa), at audit-grade Ascher verification integrity.

The five-color palette is near-even at the top. Red-brown twenty-nine percent, alpaca-brown twenty-seven. These two channels together carry fifty-six percent of the document. Medium-brown twelve percent, forest-color twelve percent, white seven percent. This is DIMENSIONAL ENCODING -- five categories with the top two taking the bulk, similar to UR198's three-color 47/34/17 distribution but with five dimensions instead of three.

At Incahuasi in Chu's JC-series, JC009 is about twice the scale of JC001 (thirty-six cords, 6/6 Ascher audit-grade) and similar to JC013 (seventy-eight cords, verification + construction register, 2/9 Ascher partial). It is fifteen times smaller than JC010 (three hundred and seventy-four cords, large sparse audit). If JC009 + JC010 form a compound-document pair -- the 100-percent-Ascher-verified compact audit (JC009) paired with the 95-percent-Ascher-verified large sparse register (JC010) -- the relationship would parallel UR273A/UR273B (compact event-log + large audit-totals) or AS026A/AS026B (compact audit + lineage roll call).

Without specific OKR notes on JC009 or JC010, the compound-pairing hypothesis is structural only. But the co-provenance (both Incahuasi Chu JC-series), similar Ascher cascade depth (4), and complementary structural scales suggest a possible physical or administrative association.

Ninety-five cords. Six words distributed across five dimensional colours. Eighteen verified sums at one hundred percent integrity. The audit-grade cadastral verification of some specific Incahuasi parcel or tribute, preserved in Chu's field-curation archive since the 2013+ excavation campaign.

prior_work.claim: Chu 2013+ Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi produced a numbered JC-prefix khipu series. JC009 sits within this series with adjacent audit-grade peers (JC001 6/6, JC011 5/5) and vocabulary-rich peers (JC012 qaqa x12 toponym index, JC013 verification + construction 4 STRING).
prior_work.citation: Chu, A. (2013+). Proyecto Arqueologico Incahuasi excavation reports -- JC-series.

prior_work.claim: Audit-grade Ascher verification (>= 80 percent) distinguishes audit-tier documents from day-book tier. Corpus audit-grade class includes UR275, AS026A, AS092, JC011, HP018, JC001, and (now) JC009.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint; Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse.

prior_work.claim: Canonical cadastral-audit vocabulary combines cadastral markers (qaqa, taqa, kaqa, paqa) with audit markers (chiqa, maqa). JC009's 4-marker signature (qaqa + taqa + chiqa + maqa) is a balanced cadastral-audit signature.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Cadastral-audit vocabulary corpus analysis.

v3_contribution.claim: 18/18 ASCHER VERIFIED = 100 PERCENT AUDIT-GRADE at CASCADE DEPTH 4. Peer to corpus elite: AS092 (18/18 depth 5), AS050 (42/48 depth 6), UR275 (5/5), AS026A (4/4), JC011 (5/5), HP018 (7/7), JC001 (6/6). JC009 is the 8th corpus audit-grade document.

evidence.test: 18-of-18 Ascher pendant-pendant sum verification at cascade depth 4

v3_contribution.claim: BALANCED 4-MARKER CADASTRAL-AUDIT SIGNATURE: maqa x2 + taqa x2 (doubled accounting markers) + qaqa + chiqa (single identifier markers). First corpus instance of this exact 4-marker double-double signature on a single register.

POTENTIAL PAIRING WITH JC010: JC009 (95 cords, 100-percent Ascher at depth 4) + JC010 (374 cords, 95-percent Ascher at depth 4) share (a) Chu Incahuasi provenance, (b) similar cascade depth, (c) complementary scales. JC009 may be compact audit + JC010 large audited register pair, paralleling UR273A/B and AS026A/B compound-document conventions.
DENSE REGISTER (only 6 percent empty) distinguishes JC009 from the pre-formatted template class (AS050 57 percent, UR273A 74 percent). JC009 is a FULLY-USED cadastral-audit register, not a template.

companions.note: CO-PROVENANCE POTENTIAL PAIR: JC010 (374 cords, 1 STRING qaqa, 20/21 Ascher = 95 percent at cascade depth 4). Possible compound-document pair: JC009 compact audit + JC010 large audited register. Testable by physical-association lookup.
Chu first catalogue audit-grade (36 cords, 6/6 Ascher, 5-color balanced). JC009 is the larger cadastral-audit peer with vocabulary content.
Chu Sector B measurements (126 cords, 12 colors, 5/5 Ascher audit-grade). Same JC-series + audit-grade class. JC009 and JC011 are two of the corpus's 8 audit-grade documents.
Chu Sector B verification + construction register (78 cords, 4 STRING maqa+chiqa+paki+chi-DIR, 2/9 Ascher partial). Similar 4-marker verification vocabulary; JC009 is the audit-grade variant.
Gaffron Pachacamac 10x6 audit matrix (56 cords, 18/18 Ascher, cascade depth 5). Audit-grade peer at slightly deeper cascade.
Incahuasi UE 19 peanut audit (5/5 Ascher, 47-constant). Audit-grade peer at smaller scale; UR275 anchors commodity (peanut); JC009 anchors cadastral-verification.
Incahuasi UE 14 audit totals (124 cords, no Ascher layer, large audit magnitudes). Paired-document half convention at Incahuasi.
Pachacamac verification register (132 cords, chiqa x6 corpus record). JC009's chiqa + cadastral vocabulary parallels UR196's verification-cascade convention.

word_reading.note: 6/6 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. maqa + taqa + qaqa + chiqa all canonical cadastral-audit markers.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey audit-grade sub-type robustly supported by (a) 4-marker balanced signature, (b) 18/18 Ascher = 100 percent, (c) cascade depth 4, (d) Chu JC-series provenance, (e) dense register structure, (f) 5-color dimensional encoding. Sub-type: cadastral-verification audit with tally marks.

narrative.note: The 'substantial cadastral-audit register with balanced 4-marker vocabulary + 100-percent Ascher verification' reading is internally coherent and multi-anchored. Possible pairing with JC010 is structural hypothesis.

data_integrity.note: 18/18 Ascher verified + cascade depth 4 + 6 percent empty = audit-grade integrity profile. Chu JC-series provenance precise.

sections.title: Balanced Cadastral-Audit Register
sections.reading: 95 cords across 8 clusters + 5-color palette (RB 29 + AB 27 + MB 13 + FR 12 + W 7 percent). 83 INT + 6 STRING (maqa x2 + taqa x2 + qaqa + chiqa) + 6 EMPTY. 18/18 Ascher verified = audit-grade cadastral-verification. Balanced 4-marker signature tracks 2 boundaries + 2 tally marks + 1 rock landmark + 1 truth verification.
